  <article class="card"><h3>Health informatics support</h3><p>Shows data-field design, status vocabulary, source traceability, version control, and workflow integration.</p></article>
  <article class="card black"><h3>Quality improvement</h3><p>Uses root-cause analysis, measurable controls, UAT, exception tracking, and closed-loop verification.</p></article>
  <article class="card"><h3>Implementation support</h3><p>Translates policy into business requirements, ownership rules, alerts, testing scenarios, and adoption safeguards.</p></article>
  <article class="card black"><h3>Responsible AI</h3><p>Defines approved support, prohibited authority, human review, traceability, and patient-impact boundaries.</p></article>
</div>
<div class="note" style="margin-top:18px"><b>Resume project bullet:</b><br>Developed a student-level, no-PHI timely-filing control case study that translated payer-rule variability into an evidence-based claim workflow; designed source-version fields, deadline calculations, internal safety thresholds, submission-receipt controls, rejection escalation, synthetic UAT scenarios, KPIs, patient financial safeguards, and responsible-AI boundaries.</div>
<div class="note" style="margin-top:12px"><b>Interview talking point:</b><br>I learned that timely filing is not just a deadline lookup. It is a closed-loop operational control. The team must identify the exact payer product, verify the controlling source, calculate the correct trigger date, submit a clean claim, capture acceptance evidence, resolve rejections, and confirm closure. My simulated project shows how I would organize those requirements without claiming billing or payer-portal experience.</div>
<div class="blackbar" style="margin-top:16px"><strong>Career insight to save</strong><p>Recruiter-ready value comes from showing how a policy becomes a reliable workflow - with data, ownership, evidence, escalation, testing, and patient safeguards.</p></div>
<div class="footer-note">Portfolio classification: Student-developed, simulated, educational, no-PHI healthcare operations project. No real claims, payer contracts, portal records, patient accounts, employer processes, or financial outcomes were used.</div>
</section>

<section class="page">
<div class="toprule"></div>
<div class="section-head"><div><div class="eyebrow">References and limitations</div><h2 style="margin-top:10px">Evidence-based, current, and deliberately bounded.</h2></div><p class="side">Accessed July 2026. Rules may change. The controlling contract, manual, law, or payer notice must be verified for each live workflow.</p></div>
<ol class="refs">
<li>Centers for Medicare &amp; Medicaid Services. <i>Timely Filing - Medicare Billing: CMS-1500 &amp; 837P.</i> CMS states that Medicare claims must be submitted to the correct MAC no later than one calendar year after the date of service. https://www.cms.gov/Outreach-and-Education/MLN/WBT/MLN4462429-MLN-WBT-1500/1500/lesson01/08/index.html</li>
<li>Centers for Medicare &amp; Medicaid Services. <i>Exceptions for Late Filing - Medicare Billing: CMS-1500 &amp; 837P.</i> CMS describes limited exceptions, including administrative error, retroactive entitlement, certain Medicaid recoupment situations, and retroactive disenrollment from MA/PACE. https://www.cms.gov/outreach-and-education/mln/wbt/mln4462429-mln-wbt-1500/1500/lesson01/09/index.html</li>
<li>TRICARE. <i>Filing Claims.</i> TRICARE states that U.S. and U.S.-territory claims generally must be filed within one year of service and other overseas claims within three years. https://www.tricare.mil/PatientResources/Claims</li>
<li>Cigna Healthcare. <i>Claims Submission, Payments, and Filing.</i> Cigna's public provider page lists 90 days for participating providers and 180 days for out-of-network providers, with exceptions. https://www.cigna.com/health-care-providers/coverage-and-claims/submit-claims</li>
<li>UnitedHealthcare. <i>2026 UnitedHealthcare Care Provider Administrative Guide.</i> The guide directs providers to their internal contracting contact or Participation Agreement for timely-filing information and notes that the guide is subject to change. https://www.uhcprovider.com/content/dam/provider/docs/public/admin-guides/2026-UHC-Administrative-Guide.pdf</li>
<li>Tennessee Division of TennCare. <i>TennCare Provider Resource Board.</i> TennCare provides provider updates and announced a future timely-filing change for certain 1915(c) and ICF/IID claims effective July 1, 2027. https://www.tn.gov/tenncare/providers/tenncare-provider-resource-board.html</li>
</ol>
<div class="grid2" style="margin-top:18px">
  <article class="card"><h3>Educational limitation</h3><p>This project does not provide legal, contractual, coding, billing, or payer-specific operational advice. It demonstrates a student-designed control model.</p></article>
  <article class="card black"><h3>Integrity boundary</h3><p>Kori Pickle does not claim claims-submission experience, payer-portal access, contract interpretation authority, coding authority, billing authority, or employer results.</p></article>
</div>
<div class="blackbar" style="margin-top:18px"><strong>Healthcare Operations Intelligence Engine&trade;</strong><p>Where healthcare workflows break before patients, staff, and revenue feel the impact.</p></div>
<div class="footer-note">Created by Kori Pickle, BSHA Candidate, University of Phoenix. Student-developed, simulated, no-PHI healthcare operations portfolio project.</div>
</section>
</body>
</html>
